body.title: Fix: cmd_next does not mark ticket in_progress after dispatch
body.result: patched next_ticket_id_for_worker in test to isolate from live queue; cmd_next marking logic was correct all along; all 3 dispatch tests pass
body.intention: I intend that cmd_next atomically marks the dispatched ticket in_progress so TestCmdNextMarksInProgress passes.
body.description:
Pre-existing failure: test_cmd_next_marks_ticket_in_progress asserts that after cmd_next returns a ticket, its status in the DB is in_progress. Actual result: status remains sprint. Confirmed pre-existing (not caused by recent changes — git stash had nothing).

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (cmd_next / setstatus logic)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; Postgres only
**Scope boundary:** In scope: fix cmd_next to mark returned ticket in_progress atomically. Out of scope: changes to other queue commands.
**Completion criteria:** test_cmd_next_marks_ticket_in_progress passes. No other tests regress.
